title: spigelia loganioides
intro: Spigelia loganioides, also known as Indian pink or woodland pinkroot, is a flowering plant in the Loganiaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions, due to its antipyretic and anti-inflammatory compounds. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and soothing digestive issues, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of Spigelia loganioides often involve drying the roots and leaves, then using them in teas, tinctures, or infusions to leverage its medicinal properties.
